subject: Accounting
title: Why Ethics Matter in Accounting
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Why Ethics Matter in Accounting.

hook.text: One dishonest accountant can destroy thousands of jobs, wipe out savings, and crash markets.

buildup.text: Accounting relies on trust. Investors, banks, and regulators all depend on honest numbers.

discovery.text: Without ethical standards, financial statements become worthless—nobody could believe any report.

twist.text: The profession's reputation was built over centuries but can be shattered by a single scandal.

climax.text: Ethics aren't optional in accounting—they're the foundation the entire system stands on.

punchline.text: Trust is accounting's most valuable asset.

quiz.question: Why are ethics especially important in accounting?
options: Because the entire financial system depends on honest reporting
